title: Larian Studios backs off from gen AI, says tech won’t be used in new Divinity - Niche Gamer
text:
After a few weeks of consistent backlash, Larian Studios has sworn off generative AI use, at least for their next game – the brand new
Divinity
game.
Promised a few weeks ago, Larian CEO Swen Vincke hosted
an AMA on Reddit
where they talked up the upcoming Divinity game and of course – the controversial use of gen AI. It seems like the backlash worked as Larian is swearing off gen AI.
“First off – there is not going to be any GenAI art in Divinity,” Vincke said in the AMA. “I know there’s been a lot of discussion about us using AI tools as part of concept art exploration. We already said this doesn’t mean the actual concept art is generated by AI but we understand it created confusion.”
Furthermore, the team behind the new Divinity is also dropping any gen AI tools during development so there’s “no room for doubt” while they’re making the game.
While this may seem like a resounding victory for anti-gen AI folks, the Larian boss was quick to note that gen AI will allow their team to iterate and experiment more in development.
“We continuously try to improve the speed with which we can try things out,” Vincke said. “The more iterations we can do, the better in general the gameplay is.”
Previously, the Larian boss was
misquoted over their purported gen AI use on games
, which led to the initial backlash, only to then
double down on gen AI being a promising tool
– when used right. Swen’s latest comments echo his remarks from weeks ago.
“We think GenAI can help with this and so we’re trying things out across departments,” Vincke said in the AMA. “Our hope is that it can aid us to refine ideas faster, leading to a more focused development cycle, less waste, and ultimately, a higher-quality game.”
Most of what Vincke has been talking up with gen AI may sound a bit nebulous but overall it sounds like they want to use gen AI to experiment and iterate more, not use it as filler for in-game assets.
“The important bit to note is that we will not generate ‘creative assets’ that end up in a game without being 100% sure about the origins of the training data and the consent of those who created the data,” he added. “If we use a GenAI model to create in-game assets, then it’ll be trained on data we own.”
Larian’s new
Divinity
game is
actively in development
though a release date and release platforms weren’t confirmed.
Divinity (2026)
,
Larian Studios
